data.subject: The Whip's Weekly Preview: Friday, July 17, 2026
data.weekAheadText:
Monday, July 20, 2026
On Monday, the House will meet at 12:00 p.m. for Morning Hour debate and 2:00 p.m. for legislative business, with votes postponed until 6:30 p.m.

Suspensions (24 bills)

  1. H.R. 8720 – Campaign Finance Transparency Act, as amended (Rep. Steil – House Administration)
  2. H.R. 2715 – Destruction of Hazardous Imports Act, as amended (Rep. Higgins – Energy and Commerce)
  3. H.R. 2821 – FDA Modernization Act 3.0 (Rep. Carter (GA) – Energy and Commerce)
  4. H.R. 4348 – To reauthorize the Kay Hagan Tick Act (Rep. Smith (NJ) – Energy and Commerce)
  5. H.R. 5160 – Stem Cell Therapeutic and Research Reauthorization Act of 2025, as amended (Rep. Smith (NJ) – Energy and Commerce) 
  6. H.R. 3747 – Accelerating Access to Dementia and Alzheimer’s Provider Training (AADAPT) Act, as amended (Rep. Balderson – Energy and Commerce)
  7. H.R. 8209 – School-Based Health Centers Reauthorization Act of 2026 (Rep. Tonko – Energy and Commerce)
  8. H.R. 2001 – Action for Dental Health Act, as amended (Rep. Kelly (IL) – Energy and Commerce)
  9. H.R. 1493 – To reauthorize and make improvements to Federal programs relating to the prevention, detection, and treatment of traumatic brain injuries, as amended (Rep. Pallone – Energy and Commerce)
  10. H.R. 3491 – DeOndra Dixon INCLUDE Project Act of 2025 (Rep. DeGette – Energy and Commerce)
  11. H.R. 4541 – EARLY Act Reauthorization of 2025, as amended (Rep. Wasserman Schultz – Energy and Commerce)
  12. H.R. 6238 – NIH IMPROVE Act, as amended (Rep. Underwood – Energy and Commerce) 
  13. H.R. 8205 – Accelerating Access to Critical Therapies for ALS Reauthorization Act of 2026, as amended (Rep. Quigley – Energy and Commerce)
  14. H.R. 1703 – Choices for Increased Mobility Act of 2026, as amended (Rep. Joyce (PA) – Energy and Commerce)
  15. H.R. 8823 – Putting Patients First by Strengthening Provider Accountability in FECA Act, as amended (Rep. Mackenzie – Education and Workforce)
  16. H.R. 1118 – Value Over Cost Act of 2026, as amended (Rep. Donalds – Oversight and Government Reform)
  17. H.R. 7283 – Ensuring Federal Purchasing Efficiency Act (Rep. Fallon – Oversight and Government Reform)
  18. H.R. 2069 – Stop Secret Spending Act of 2025 (Rep. Moore (AL) – Oversight and Government Reform)
  19. H.R. 4123 – FIT Procurement Act, as amended (Rep. Burlison – Oversight and Government Reform)
  20. H.R. 6330 – Federal Relocation Payment Improvement Act, as amended (Rep. Jack – Oversight and Government Reform)
  21. H.R. 1722 – Billion Dollar Boondoggle Act, as amended (Rep. Miller-Meeks – Oversight and Government Reform)
  22. H.R. 5578 – Expanding Whistleblower Protections for Contractors Act of 2026, as amended (Rep. Garcia (CA) – Oversight and Government Reform)
  23. H.R. 3029 – Nucleic Acid Standards for Biosecurity Act, as amended (Rep. Salinas – Science, Space, and Technology)
  24. H.R. 8790 – Next-Generation Geothermal Research and Development Act, as amended (Rep. Harrigan – Science, Space, and Technology)

Tuesday, July 21, 2026 and the Balance of the Week
On Tuesday, the House will meet at 10:00 a.m. for Morning Hour and 12:00 p.m. for legislative business. On Wednesday, the House will meet at 10:00 a.m. for legislative business. On Thursday, the House will meet at 9:00 a.m. for legislative business, with last votes expected no later than 3:00 p.m.

items.title: Take Care of America’s Veterans Act (Rep. Bost – Veterans' Affairs) (Subject to a Rule)
items.rawText: H.R. 9237 – Take Care of America’s Veterans Act (Rep. Bost – Veterans' Affairs) (Subject to a Rule) – VOTE NO

data.sourceText:
“One Minutes” (5 per side)

H.R. 9237 – Take Care of America’s Veterans Act (Rep. Bost – Veterans' Affairs) (Subject to a Rule) – VOTE NO

This bill purports to increase benefits for veterans and improve services offered by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). In reality, it would undercut critical care and benefits for those who have dedicated their lives to serving this country. 

House Republicans have chosen to put forward a bill with no input or buy-in from House Democrats that hijacks bipartisan priorities while advancing their own partisan policy provisions. These provisions will undercut the VA care and benefits veterans rely on day-to-day. While House Democrats are working to ensure veterans have easy access to their disability benefits and treatment options, cutting certain benefits for one group of veterans to fund new benefits for another is not the answer. Unfortunately, this bill does exactly that – cutting future benefits for 1.5 million veterans with tinnitus and sleep apnea to cover the cost of benefits for other veterans. Those savings do not come from waste or bureaucracy. They come directly from veterans.

The bill also contains large fee increases to VA home loan programs that veterans rely on for their housing needs. Ultimately, this bill would slash approximately $57 billion over the next 10 years in veterans earned disability compensation and $4 billion in home loan benefits and is opposed by a wide coalition – including several Veteran Service Organizations. 

House Democrats have filed a bipartisan discharge petition that would bring the Major Richard Star Act to the Floor, which would ensure medically retired veterans receive their full Department of Defense retirement pay and their full VA disability pay without cutting the benefits of other veterans. House Republicans should join this effort to advance the needs of the veteran community – without harming it in the process. 

Ranking Member Takano strongly opposes this bill.

The Rule, which was adopted on Tuesday, provides for one hour of debate equally divided and controlled by the Chair and Ranking Member of the Committee on Veterans' Affairs or their respective designees.

Possible Postponed Suspension (1 vote)

  1. H.R. 5362 – To name the VA clinic in Marietta, GA, as the “Colonel Michael H. Boyce Department of Veterans Affairs Multispecialty Clinic" (Rep. Loudermilk – Veterans' Affairs)
